symbols.name: QueueEntry
symbols.detail: [OK] Well-formed interface; both fields are structurally sound.

symbols.name: compareTrains
symbols.detail: [NEEDS_FIX] insertionOrder is declared in QueueEntry and is never consulted in the comparator; when two entries tie on trainId (possible if trainId encodes a shared priority class, as the README implies with "priority class"), the comparator returns 0 and queue ordering becomes non-deterministic instead of FIFO.

findings.detail: The tie-break branch (return 0) ignores insertionOrder. A stable FIFO dispatch among equal-priority trains requires a secondary sort: if (a.trainId === b.trainId) return a.insertionOrder - b.insertionOrder.

actions.description: Add FIFO tie-breaking: when a.trainId === b.trainId, return a.insertionOrder - b.insertionOrder so trains of equal priority are dispatched in arrival order.
